hypothesis: SpecAugment (time+freq masking, 2 masks per axis, time≤30 frames ≈300ms, freq≤8 of 64 bins) applied training-only. Baseline reverts to #37 operating point (ls=0, threshold=0.3) because #43+#44 established that ls shifts operating point without adding discrimination — so SpecAugment is tested cleanly here, not confounded with ls. #37 is the scientifically clean discrimination anchor; to beat prev_best #43 (0.5345, near-degenerate) requires sens+spec > 1.069 from a balanced operating point, i.e. true discrimination improvement. If it works: augmentation is a corridor-escape lever and warrants sweeping. If flat (metric near #37's 0.4910): the model at 5 epochs isn't bottlenecked by memorization and augmentation isn't the right regularizer. If sharp regression: augmentation at these mask widths destroys class-differential spectral structure (e.g. the 500-1000 Hz signal #13 surfaced).
change_description: SpecAugment on (t≤30 frames, f≤8 bins, n_masks=2); label_smoothing reverted 0.1→0.0
